programme.title: Dr. Dee: Alaska Vet
programme.desc: Animal House gets busy as Dr. Dee must deal with a Chihuahua with a life-threatening condition, trim the beak of a vocal Macaw, and treat a deathly ill newborn calf; Dr. Dee and Ken add seven fainting goats to the family at home.

programme.desc: The doctors visit their alma mater for a class reunion and become professors for a day; a shih tzu is rushed to the clinic after swallowing a large piece of rawhide, while the owner of a pit bull with a deformed leg must consider drastic surgery.

programme.title: The Vet Life
programme.desc: The doctors are honored for their contributions to the community; a goat with a twisted leg; a Great Dane with a life-threatening condition; a bulldog is rescued from a puppy mill.

programme.title: Dodo Heroes
programme.desc: Derrick Campana first began building prosthetics for humans, but one day that all changed when he built his first prosthetic for a dog, and then an elephant.

programme.sub-title: Jan and Tim's Greatest Show On Earth

programme.desc: A feisty gator in Galveston County needs a new home; shrimpers try to conceal their catch; a family's property is damaged by stray bullets; the wardens stage a boat crash.

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: In Liberty County, the death of a deer is investigated; fawns in Harris County are being illegally kept as pets; patrol on Somerville Lake leads to multiple citations; a suspicious car is stopped in Red River County.

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: A running deer must be caught before it hurts itself; the game wardens investigate an impersonation case in which a man claims to be one of their own; Richards comes across a suspicious group while he is patrolling Lake Tawakoni.

programme.desc: It's snapper season on the Gulf and the game wardens catching fishermen who are breaking the law; two men are investigated for cruelty to a wild pig; a jet ski collision leads to arguments; an exotic tortoise finds a new home.

programme.title: Yellowstone Wardens
programme.desc: Wildlife decoys lead to surprising arrests; a warden's K9 unearths critical evidence; a major investigation into moose and elk poaching results in a significant law enforcement takedown.

programme.title: Yellowstone Wardens
programme.desc: Wardens help rescue a hunter with a broken leg. Hunters coming through a warden check station get into hot water after not telling the truth.

programme.title: Yellowstone Wardens
programme.desc: Shed antler hunting nearly turns lethal for one man; training for game wardens becomes eerily realistic; one camp of unruly hunters attracts the attention of wardens; when an endangered grizzly bear is shot, wardens come out in full force.

programme.title: Yellowstone Wardens
programme.sub-title: If I Was a Bettin' Man
programme.desc: Hunters claim they weren't shooting from the road, although physical evidence proves otherwise; a warden is skeptical when a bow hunter without a license alleges he's only elk calling; hunters find out the hard way they're on private property.

programme.title: Yellowstone Wardens
programme.desc: Anglers find out that using too many poles will land them a hefty fine.

programme.title: Wardens of the North
programme.desc: Officers patrol elk hunters and regulate the elk population, teach outdoor skills to kids at a pocket park, enforce traffic laws and monitor ice fishing activity and ice conditions.

programme.title: Wardens of the North
programme.desc: The lives of men and women who are making the Michigan wilderness and landscape a safer place for not only animals, but humans alike.

programme.title: Wardens of the North
programme.desc: Michigan conservation officers monitor elk hunters across early and late seasons; a blind hunter secures a prized bear tag with help from a group of kids and veterans; officers patrol snowmobilers and ice anglers braving frigid conditions.

programme.title: Wardens of the North
programme.desc: Officers patrol archery and firearm deer seasons, monitor ORV use, tag suburban ducks, and investigate a buck shot during a coyote hunt, while hunters donate venison to help those in need. Follow Michigan Conservation Officers as they protect and patrol the most precious natural resources in one of the nation's most remote areas.

programme.desc: Officers patrol elk hunters and regulate the elk population, teach outdoor skills to kids at a pocket park, enforce traffic laws and monitor ice fishing activity and ice conditions.

programme.title: Pets & Pickers
programme.desc: Motivated by the extraordinary compassion and belief that all animals deserve treatment, the vet and thrift store team of the Regional Animal Protection Society work tirelessly to offer low-income pet families access to life-saving healthcare.

programme.desc: Paul and Jimmy capture a monster-sized gator; an alligator fights to protect her young.

programme.desc: Paul and Scott must fend for themselves when Jimmy makes an adventurous trip to South Carolina.

programme.desc: Reality star Adrianne Curry and her boyfriend, Todd are living with Adrianne's menacing Siamese cat, Billy Shears; a college student feels like she is being held prisoner by her bloodthirsty feline, Storm.
